provenance.title: Coordinated Sniper Cohorts on Pump.fun: Detection of 1,012 Persistent Wallet Rings and the Limits of Naive Causal Inference for First-Hour Buyer Flow
provenance.abstract_truncated: We study coordinated buyer behavior on the Solana pump.fun bonding-curve marketplace using 1,578,333 buyer observations from 166,098 token launches between 2026-06-12 and 2026-06-26. A two-stage detection pipeline identifies 1,012 persistent wallet cohorts that systematically co-fire as early buyers across multiple launches.
key_claims: A two-stage detection pipeline (intra-launch first-buyer-window + cross-launch union-find on co-occurrence graphs) identifies 1,012 persistent wallet cohorts from 166,098 pump.fun token launches.
153 cohorts qualify as high-tier (≥10 launches hit or score ≥100); 22 hit ≥20 launches each; the top cohort (9 wallets) appears in the first-10-buyer queue of 42 launches in 11 days.
Cohort-touched launches show a naive +132.3% lift in first-30-minute buyer count (95% CI [+127.0%, +137.4%]) and +136.5% lift in SOL inflow versus random-matched controls.
An activity-matched placebo yields a LARGER +216.3% lift (95% CI [+183.8%, +255.2%]), entirely above the real-cohort CI, refuting a cohort-specific causal interpretation.
The association reflects launch-quality selection bias; causal identification requires propensity-score matching on launch-quality covariates (left to future work).
provenance.patent_note: Detection methodology subject to pending US patent application No. 64/099,108 (filed 2026-06-25, Embodiment H)

architecture.description: This is an empirical data-analysis / detection pipeline, not a neural-network architecture. The 'architecture' maps to the two-stage algorithmic pipeline plus the scoring function.

components.name: PassiveSolanaObserver
components.role: Data collection layer — read-only listener on Solana chain
components.operation: Stream ingestion, sorting buyer events by blockTime per mint

components.name: Stage1_IntraLaunchExtractor
components.role: For each launch, extract ordered list of first-N buyers
outputs: per-launch index: {mint, wallet, rank, blockTime, sol_committed}
components.operation: Filter buyer events to rank ∈ [1,10] per mint; 166,098 qualifying mints

components.name: CoOccurrenceGraphBuilder
components.role: Build undirected weighted graph G=(V,E) of wallet co-occurrences
outputs: Graph G: V=wallet addresses, E=co-occurrence edges with integer weights
components.operation: Add edge (u,v) for each launch where both u and v appear in first-10 buyers; weight = count of co-occurrences across all launches

components.name: EdgeWeightFilter
components.operation: Threshold filter: retain only edges with co-occurrence count ≥ 3 (qualifying-pair threshold). Selected before reviewing buyer-flow effect to avoid threshold-hacking.

components.name: UnionFindCohortSurface
components.role: Surface connected components (candidate cohorts) from filtered graph
components.operation: Standard union-find / disjoint-set on G_filtered. Produces 1,161 raw components at cutoff=3.

components.name: CohortSizeFilter
components.role: Remove noise hubs with too many wallets
components.operation: Remove components with size > MAX_COHORT_SIZE = 12. Reduces 1,161 → 1,012 cohorts.

components.name: CohortScorer
components.role: Rank cohorts by a composite quality score
components.operation: score(C) = 10 × Σ_L 1{C touches L}  +  5 / mean_first_rank(C)  +  √(Σ_L SOL_committed(C, L))

score_components.launch_coverage_term: 10 × (number of distinct launches touched by C)
score_components.volume_term: √(total SOL committed across all touched launches)

components.score_threshold_tau: set to surface 1,012 cohorts with desired tier distribution

components.name: TierClassifier
components.role: Assign qualitative tier labels to cohorts
outputs: Tier label: Standard / High / Premium per cohort
components.operation: Premium: n_launches ≥ 20. High: n_launches ≥ 10 OR score ≥ 100. Standard: otherwise.

pipeline_flow: scored cohorts → TierClassifier → {Standard, High, Premium} labels

equations.name: Cohort Score Function

variables.L: A token launch (indexed over all launches in corpus)
variables.1{C touches L}: Indicator: at least 1 wallet in C appears among first-10 buyers of L
variables.r_bar(C): mean_first_rank(C) — mean of the minimum rank attained by any cohort wallet in each touched launch
variables.SOL_committed(C,L): Sum of SOL committed by all cohort wallets in launch L

equations.role: Composite ranking score weighting launch coverage, queue priority, and capital commitment
equations.note: Three additive sub-scores are intentionally dimensionally heterogeneous; the function is a ranking heuristic, not a probabilistic quantity

equations.name: Percentage Lift Estimator

variables.Y_treated: Outcome (buyer count or SOL inflow) for cohort-touched launches
variables.Y_control: Outcome for randomly drawn untouched launches

equations.role: Primary causal estimand (naive, before placebo refutation)

equations.name: Co-occurrence Edge Weight

variables.first10(L): Set of first 10 buyer wallets for launch L

equations.role: Graph edge weight used for filtering (retain if w ≥ 3)

tensor_semantics.note: This is a tabular / graph-theoretic pipeline. No neural tensors. Data structures described below.

primary_data_structures.name: buyer_event
primary_data_structures.schema: {mint: str, wallet: str, slot: int, blockTime: int, sol_in: float, tx_sig: str, rank: int}

primary_data_structures.name: launch_metadata
primary_data_structures.schema: {mint: str, symbol: str, name: str, created_timestamp: int, initial_mcap_sol: float, has_twitter: bool, has_website: bool, has_telegram: bool, description_len: int}

primary_data_structures.name: cohort_record
primary_data_structures.schema: {cohort_id: str, wallets: list[str], size: int, n_launches_hit: int, mean_first_rank: float, total_sol: float, score: float, first_seen: int, last_seen: int, tier: str, per_mint_hits: list[{mint, rank, blockTime, sol}]}

primary_data_structures.name: co_occurrence_graph
primary_data_structures.schema: Adjacency representation: V = unique wallet addresses (from first-10 buyers), E = pairs with weight ≥ 3

training_pipeline.note: No ML training pipeline exists. This is a deterministic algorithmic method. Equivalent fields mapped to pipeline configuration.

causal_estimation.design: 3:1 random-matched (treated vs randomly drawn untouched mints)

placebo_checks.description: 1,012 placebo cohorts sampled uniformly from buyer-event universe at same size distribution as real cohorts
placebo_checks.verdict: Placebo lift > real-cohort lift; causal interpretation weakened

placebo_checks.description: For each real cohort of size k, sample k placebo wallets from non-cohort pool individually matched on per-wallet launch-count (±100)
placebo_checks.touch_threshold: ≥2 placebo wallets (same as real-cohort strict threshold)
placebo_checks.verdict: Real-cohort lift (132.3%) is entirely BELOW placebo CI lower bound (183.8%). Zero CI overlap. Strong refutation of cohort-specific causal effect.

robustness_checks.name: top3_exclusion
robustness_checks.interpretation: Naive lift robust to outlier removal; consistent with selection effect operating across all cohort tiers

robustness_checks.name: tier_stratification
robustness_checks.verdict: Non-monotone pattern (Premium < Standard) inconsistent with 'more coordination → more flow'; further supports selection interpretation

concentration_analysis.lorenz_curve: Top 10 cohorts (≈1%) account for >9% of cohort-touch events; top 100 (≈10%) account for >35%

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Score threshold tau is set post-hoc to yield exactly 1,012 cohorts; the exact numeric value of tau is not disclosed in the paper
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states tau surfaces cohorts satisfying conditions (a)+(b)+(c) but does not give a numeric threshold

implementation_assumptions.assumption: mean_first_rank(C) in the score formula is the mean over all launches touched by C of the minimum rank attained by any cohort member in that launch
implementation_assumptions.basis: Stated as 'average first-buyer rank' for cohort in descriptive context (COH-0001 has mean_first_rank = 2.29); the exact aggregation over multiple wallets per launch is inferred
alternatives: Mean over all (wallet, launch) pairs regardless of which wallet is first

implementation_assumptions.assumption: 1{C touches L} in the score formula uses the ≥1-cohort-wallet definition (loose), not ≥2 (strict used in causal analysis)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper uses ≥1 for the detection/scoring pipeline and ≥2 for the causal sample construction; the score equation predates the causal section
alternatives: ≥2 wallet touch threshold for score computation as well

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Union-find is run with standard path-compression and union-by-rank (canonical implementation)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says 'we run union-find on the filtered graph'; standard implementation assumed

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The 15-day observation window is treated as a single static batch; no time-series or rolling-window analysis is performed
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper describes corpus as collected over 2026-06-12 to 2026-06-26 with no mention of rolling windows

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Framework: Python with pandas/networkx for graph operations and scipy/numpy for bootstrap CI computation
implementation_assumptions.basis: Script names (analyze_sniper_cohorts.py, gen_p7_artifacts.py) and standard Python data-science toolchain for this type of work

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Bootstrap CIs use numpy percentile method (2.5th and 97.5th percentiles of 1,000 resampled lift estimates) with replacement
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states 'percentile method' for bootstrap; standard interpretation is scipy/numpy percentile on resampled statistic

ambiguities.description: The exact numeric value of score threshold tau is not given. Only stated as 'set to surface cohorts satisfying (a), (b), (c)'.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: tau is implicitly around 40-50 (just below the reported median score of 52.8), or may be a min-launches cutoff
alternatives: tau chosen to yield 1,012 via binary search

ambiguities.location: Section 4.2 — cohort touch definition vs score formula
ambiguities.description: The paper uses ≥2 cohort wallets for the causal analysis (Section 6) but the score formula uses 1{C touches L} — whether this indicator uses ≥1 or ≥2 wallets is ambiguous.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Score formula uses ≥1 (looser definition); causal section uses ≥2 (stricter)

ambiguities.description: Only 173 placebo-touched mints vs 5,411 real-cohort-touched mints. This is a very small placebo sample. The placebo CI is therefore much wider than the real-cohort CI. The statistical comparison may be power-asymmetric.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Small placebo sample is a natural consequence of ≥2-wallet strict touch definition combined with lower co-occurrence probability among individually-matched random wallets
alternatives: Use looser ≥1 touch threshold for placebo to increase sample size

ambiguities.description: Buyer rank is derived by sorting per-mint events by blockTime in the collection-time pipeline. Ties at the same blockTime are not discussed.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Ties broken by transaction slot order or tx order within slot

related_work_by_author.topic: Post-rejection follow-up sampling for counterfactual outcome measurement on Solana DEX
RED-2400 dataset of rejected memecoin trading events
